_meta.purpose: Healthcare/pharma belt faction — the Last Mile Dispensary — as a counter to Trust radiation-treatment monopoly.
_meta.note: Radiation exposure from T7 fuel operations and T6 hull-decay is universal for space workers. Trust-approved treatment is the only licensed option. Last Mile produces generic alternatives and couriers suppressed medical data.

schema.home_station: string — station id or named place
schema.monopoly_angle: string — one sentence on how this represents systemic control

last_mile_dispensary.cover: Prescribed environmental-exposure treatment clinic. Registered under T3 habitat health allocation.
last_mile_dispensary.actual_function: Generic radiation-treatment synthesis, antibiotic/insulin batch production, and medical-data courier operation for leakers inside T4 and T5.

leadership.name: Dr. Nassor Vey
leadership.background: Former T4 clinical researcher, disavowed after refusing to hand over patient genetic data to Caldera.

leadership.name: Mischa Koren
leadership.background: Runs belt distribution and keeps the dispensary physically mobile to avoid T3/T5 audits.

last_mile_dispensary.internal_politics: Vey wants to publish full case-file data and break T4's clinical monopoly. Koren wants to keep dispensing and avoid Trust retaliation. The crew splits along that line every time a big shipment comes in.
captain_services: Buy generic equivalents of restricted Trust radiation treatment at below-market rates
Carry medical-data payloads for leakers inside T4 and T5
High bond unlocks T4 case-file access without going through T4
Crew radiation-exposure recovery time reduced by one rest cycle
captain_risks: Low bond or active T4 suspicion triggers a station search
Search can temporarily lockdown the dispensary and strand the captain
T5 commercial-court classification of 'patent violation' can trigger a bounty on captain's medical supplies
last_mile_dispensary.monopoly_angle: Radiation exposure from T7 fuel enrichment and T6 hull-decay is universal for space workers. Trust-approved treatment is the only licensed option; without it, workers cannot continue in space-rated jobs. Last Mile breaks that dependency by producing generic alternatives.
